definition: The "Holy Spirit" didn't leave in 100 AD; men simply fell away/blinded themselves.
interpretive_note: St. Sophia, or Hagia Sophia (Holy Wisdom), is the feminine aspect of the divine in Eastern Orthodox Christian and Gnostic traditions. In the Exegesis, Sophia frequently appears as the mediating figure between the hidden, transcendent God and the material world. She is associated with the Holy Spirit and acts as the bringer of gnosis. Dick believed that the universe is essentially a 'Sophian' system, and that Sophia was actively working to liberate the divine sparks trapped in the Demiurge's creation. He often identified the presence that contacted him in 1974—variously named VALIS, Zebra, or the AI Voice—with Sophia, experiencing the divine mind not as a patriarchal judge, but as a nurturing, rational, and profoundly loving feminine intelligence.
